audit_items.proof_trace: `ldap_create_page_control` (L145-148) and `ldap_parse_page_control` (L242-250) each dereference `ld->ld_errno` without any NULL guard on `ld`, while their sibling functions `ldap_create_page_control_value` (L63) and `ldap_parse_pageresponse_control` (L188) in the same file explicitly check `ld == NULL`. This inconsistency means a NULL session handle passed by any caller triggers a null pointer dereference and process crash.
audit_items.attack_scenario: If `ldap_init()` returns NULL (e.g., under memory pressure or connection exhaustion) and the caller does not check before invoking `ldap_create_page_control` or `ldap_parse_page_control`, the immediate dereference of `ld->ld_errno` (L146/L243) crashes the process, enabling a remote DoS when a network attacker can trigger the NULL-init condition.
